candidates.direction_name: Restore The Best Measured PTX Grouping Window On The Accepted Surface
ranking_notes: The round-39 PTX reopen closed as a clean regression, so the loop should re-anchor before taking another branch.
Restoring the exact `489574e` surface remains the fastest path back to the accepted plateau before the 5-round checkpoint.
The restore family is still the highest-confidence dispatch whenever the active PTX branch drifts or a reopen closes as a loss.
candidates.hypothesis: Round 39 replayed the bounded PTX wait/commit-window reopen and regressed from 24.17151993 ms to 24.20684814 ms even though the headline scheduler counters stayed broadly flat. That is enough negative evidence to stop spending another immediate round on the same micro-retime family. The highest-value move for round 40 is therefore to restore the exact accepted `489574e` PTX surface again, re-anchor the search on the clean plateau, and only then decide whether the next post-checkpoint branch should reopen a different family or escalate back to a structural pivot.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Source drift away from the accepted PTX hot-band steady state after a measured-loss reopen, not DRAM saturation and not an immediate need for another same-family micro-retime.
candidates.risk: Low. The exact restore surface is already buildable and previously measured as the accepted best, so this is a bounded recovery action rather than a novel correctness risk.
metrics_to_recheck: end-to-end median runtime versus 24.20684814 ms and the accepted 24.16427231 ms anchor
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the exact restore no longer reproduces the expected plateau or if the restored source unexpectedly fails build/correctness on the current tree.

candidates.direction_name: Transplant The Half-Panel Register Budget Into The Correct 256x128 Pivot
ranking_notes: Keep the geometry pivot near the top because the PTX plateau still offers limited upside.
It ranks behind restore for round 40 because the loop should hit the checkpoint from a clean anchor instead of from a regressed branch.
candidates.hypothesis: Once the restore anchor is back in place, the strongest non-local family is still the 256x128 pivot. The PTX steady-state branch remains trapped near the same plateau, so this structural pivot should stay near the top of the queue after the checkpoint rather than another immediate replay of the just-regressed wait/commit-window tweak.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Structural latency hiding and register/shared-memory amortization on the hot band, not another tiny PTX-local ordering tweak.
candidates.risk: High. This branch materially changes the dominant hot-band geometry and has historically carried large regression risk.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the 256x128 pivot still lands in the large-regression regime or if it requires broader writer-ownership surgery than the bounded transplant plan allows.

candidates.direction_name: Restore Accepted Grouped-Rows-8 Hot-Band Consumer Ordering
ranking_notes: Keep the grouped-rows=8 family visible because it is a different PTX-side branch than the just-closed wait/commit-window reopen.
It stays third because round 40 should finish on the deterministic restore and leave new branch exploration for after the checkpoint.
candidates.hypothesis: The grouped-rows=8 consumer-ordering surface is still one of the few materially different PTX-side alternatives that has not been replayed since the most recent restore/reopen sequence. It belongs behind the exact restore and the 256x128 pivot, but it should stay visible as the next PTX-side branch once the checkpoint completes.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Grouped-row traversal and consumer-order locality in the PTX hot-band path, not the already-closed wait/commit-window retime family.
candidates.risk: Moderate to high. This branch changes CTA grouping and consumer locality and already has one historical loss, so it should remain a bounded replay rather than become the immediate fallback over the exact restore.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the grouped-rows=8 replay still lands materially behind the restored PTX anchor.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
